step_by_step_thinking: I agree that the correct answer is C: Squatting. When a child experiences an episode of hypoxia due to tetralogy of Fallot, adopting a squatting position can help increase venous return to the heart and improve pulmonary blood flow, reducing symptoms such as cyanosis. Additionally, this posture also decreases intrathoracic pressure, further aiding in better circulation to the lungs. Therefore, my answer remains C. Analysis complete.
